title: @three-ws/tour curriculum
description: The document that drives a guided tour: what the 3D guide visits and what it says. Hand-author it, or generate it with buildCurriculum().

generatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of when the curriculum was generated (optional).

tagline.description: Short subtitle shown on a tour landing page.

estimatedMinutes.description: Estimated length of the full tour.

tracks.description: Selectable views over the stops. 'full' visits every stop; 'quick' visits only highlighted stops. At least one track is recommended.

id.description: Track id. 'full' and 'quick' have built-in playlist semantics.

sections.description: Chapter headers, in render order. Each stop's `section` references a section `id`. Optional — if omitted, chapters are inferred from the stops.

intro.description: Spoken bridge said when the guide first enters this chapter.

stops.description: The ordered list of features the guide visits.

id.description: Stable id for the stop (e.g. a slug of the path).

path.description: Route the stop lives on, e.g. '/pricing'. The tour navigates here when needed; '/' and trailing-slash variants are normalized.

section.description: Section id this stop belongs to.

title.description: Short feature name shown in the chapter map.

narration.description: The line the guide speaks at this stop.

highlight.description: Whether this stop is part of the Quick-highlights track.

sectionIntro.description: If set, spoken once before this stop as the chapter's bridge (usually only on the first stop of a section).

targets.description: Ordered CSS selectors for the on-page element to spotlight. The first visible match wins; if none match, the guide falls back to the page heading / primary CTA. You can also mark an element in your HTML with [data-tour-target].
